matches.name: Alphabet (Google)
matches.description: Dominant advertising platform, network-effect moat, cyclical exposure, founder-led with super-voting structure. Closest direct peer.

matches.name: Apple
matches.description: Dominant consumer platform, switching-cost moat, regulatory headwinds, capital-discipline leadership. Different category, similar archetype.

matches.name: Microsoft
matches.description: Dominant platform incumbent in mid-pivot (cloud), wide ecosystem moat, founder-still-influential governance, willing to spend ahead of revenue.

matches.name: Amazon
matches.description: Founder-led, scale moat, multiple optionality vectors (e-commerce + AWS), willing to suppress near-term margin for long-term position.

matches.name: Netflix
matches.description: Founder-led, subscription network effects, content arms race, regulatory-light. Demonstrates 'wide-moat in cyclical demand' pattern.

matches.name: Nokia
matches.description: Once-dominant consumer platform that lost market position to a category redefinition (smartphone). Cautionary parallel for 'dominance + cyclicality' archetype.

pullquote_html: Meta's nearest neighbours are the dominant platforms of the prior cycle. The cautionary case (Nokia) lost on a feature shift the engine cannot see in advance — category redefinition rather than competitive position. The risk for Meta is the equivalent: attention shifting off feed-based social entirely.
